topic: Consolidation: dual-pass review rubric for Mode 5 frozen-prediction EXPs
summary: Operator note (created 2026-05-01T12:28:54.696Z) defines a dual-pass review rubric (Pass 1 Absolute, Pass 2 Consistency, Pass 3 Trap) to be applied to incoming Mode 5 frozen-prediction EXPs before OTS anchoring. Acceptance: the next such EXP (eclipse-2026-08-12 from analyst HNOTE-OPERATOR-MODE5-ECLIPSE-FROZEN-PRED-001) reviewed under this rubric. That review was completed by the prior curmudgeon run and is captured in EXP-283-proposal.json. This file consolidates the question-to-review mapping per Step 0c coverage-already-exists exception and marks the note consumed.

covering_review.decider_followup: Six moderate findings already opened as ISS-1801 through ISS-1806 in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json. Decider headline: 'DO NOT OTS-ANCHOR YET. Send back to analyst for one revision pass. Re-route to curmudgeon for confirmation review after revisions.'

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Pass 1 — Absolute: Is each numerical claim sourced and derivable, on its own merits, without reference to our wider review? Units correct? Confidence bounds present? Falsification criteria actually falsifiable? Symmetric falsification (dome AND standard physics AND our own analysis)?
specific_findings_examples: P1-SIGN: B1-M-001 positive amplitudes vs B2-M-001 signed negative — sign convention inconsistent (moderate)
P1-OBS-O-03: candidate non-dome-cited stations not pre-listed (moderate)
P1-AMP-FUZZ: B1-M-001 'commonly' qualifier weakens hard prediction (minor)
P1-DERIV-PROSE: B2-G-001 derivation has rounding mismatch in displayed inputs (minor)
P1-TOL: B2-M-001 falsification tolerance not committed (minor)

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Pass 2 — Consistency: Does every claim in the frozen file match the corresponding claim in our live repo at the pinned commit SHA? Numbers, units, citations, prose convergence?
specific_findings_examples: P2-RP: Internal R_p multiplier inconsistency between B2-G-002 (declared 42.4 km), B2-G-004 (uses R_p=42 -> 10.6x, 97.2x), and the implied multipliers (moderate)

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Pass 3 — Trap: Hostile-reader-defensible scrub. Peripheral claims that don't support trilemma? Ranges too narrow? Deterministic phrasing where literature shows variability? Unstated assumptions a hostile reader could exploit?
specific_findings_examples: P3-OBS-O-01-VACUOUS: OBS-O-01 partly vacuous on geometric bucket; over-aggressive in magnetic overlap zone (minor)
P3-PROJ-CONV: projection convention mentioned only in source_provenance and robustness_check (minor)
P3-OBSCURATION-DERIVE: Hartland 88% derivation not shown in frozen file (minor)
P3-V51-FROZEN: dome-version-drift policy not committed for V51.x != V51.1 between freeze and event (minor)
P3-SUMMARY-COVERAGE: B1-G-003 and B1-G-004 (semi-diameter inputs) omitted from summary table (minor)

step_0c_exception_check.decision: Coverage-already-exists exception applies. Mark note consumed via consolidation.

anti_staleness_check.method: Verified EXP-283-frozen-pred-eclipse-20260812.json has only one git commit (d9dac51, 2026-05-02T01:05:47Z) — analyst has not issued a revision since the curmudgeon review at 02:52Z. The 6 moderate findings are still active as open issues ISS-1801-1806. The covering review is current and not superseded.

alternative_view: Counter-argument: the operator note is dual-purpose. The acceptance criterion is one-time (eclipse review), but the body explicitly says 'this rubric applies to ANY Mode 5 frozen-prediction EXP, not just the eclipse one.' If we mark the note consumed, the rubric has no durable home — future Mode 5 EXPs (other eclipses, controlled experiments, etc.) would either need a fresh human note OR rely on a future curmudgeon run remembering the EXP-283 precedent. A more conservative move would be to leave the note pending until the rubric is migrated into a reference file (e.g., monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-frozen-pred-review.md). On balance the note's explicit ACCEPTANCE clause names a single artifact and that artifact has been reviewed; leaving the note pending forever is also wrong. The right move is consume + raise the durable-rubric recommendation to the operator (see recommendations_to_operator below). Reasonable reviewer could disagree.

recommendations_to_operator.title: Migrate dual-pass rubric into a reference file so future Mode 5 EXPs trigger it without a fresh human note
recommendations_to_operator.detail: The note explicitly scopes the rubric beyond eclipse-2026-08-12 ('ANY Mode 5 frozen-prediction EXP'). Recommended target: monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-frozen-pred-review.md, with a one-line trigger added to curmudgeon.md Step 0b: 'For target_type proposal where the underlying EXP has requires_frozen_prediction_review: true, read the frozen-pred rubric and apply Pass 1 / Pass 2 / Pass 3 instead of the standard proposal review.' Without this migration, when the next Mode 5 EXP arrives (per the note's NOTE ON OPERATING SCOPE), it may receive a standard curmudgeon proposal review rather than the dual-pass treatment the operator commissioned. EXP-283 happened to be reviewed correctly because (a) the human note was open at the time and (b) the prior curmudgeon run noticed the note. That's brittle.

recommendations_to_operator.title: Re-route to curmudgeon for confirmation review after analyst addresses ISS-1801-1806
recommendations_to_operator.detail: Per the EXP-283-proposal.json verdict_for_decider, curmudgeon should re-apply Pass 2 (Consistency) only after the analyst issues a revised EXP-283. Pass 1 and Pass 3 do not need to be re-run unless the revision adds new claims. The decider should push the revised EXP to the priority queue with target_type proposal so this confirmation pass happens before OTS anchoring.
recommendations_to_operator.owner_suggestion: decider (next priority-queue push when analyst delivers revisions)

kernel_of_truth.description: The operator's rubric formalization is well-considered: separating Absolute / Consistency / Trap into distinct passes prevents the common reviewer failure mode of conflating 'is this internally consistent' (Pass 2) with 'is this correct in isolation' (Pass 1) and 'will a hostile reader find this exploitable' (Pass 3). The OTS-permanence framing for severity ('a typo is critical because the anchor preserves it forever') is also a sharp prior the standard severity scale doesn't encode.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this is a meta-review of the rubric's coverage, not an attack on a dome claim.

citations_verified: EXP-283-proposal.json — read in full, rubric_passes_applied confirmed
EXP-283-frozen-pred-eclipse-20260812.json — confirmed requires_frozen_prediction_review: true and only one git commit
ISS-1801 through ISS-1806 — confirmed open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json with source: curmudgeon
